axes.name: Factual accuracy
axes.description: Are all hard facts (founding year, HQ, total raised, last round, founders, employee count) consistent with the canonical sources? Penalize hallucinated values, swapped units, or unsupported claims.

axes.name: Prior engagement coherence
axes.description: Does the brief surface what's NEW since last contact and lead with new_highlights? Penalize introducing the company from zero when prior interactions exist.

axes.name: Question sharpness
axes.description: Are key_engagement_questions specific, partner-actionable, and tied to the company's actual business model — not generic VC questions? Penalize 'what's your TAM' style fluff.

axes.name: Citation discipline
axes.description: Are non-obvious claims attributed to a source — Specter, Crunchbase, PitchBook, Attio, or a web citation? Penalize unsourced 'industry sources say' claims.

axes.name: Language calibration
axes.description: Is the prose analytical and hedged where appropriate ('Revenue est. ~$300M, modeled' not 'Revenue is $300M'; 'Founders have prior exit' not 'World-class team')? Penalize founder hagiography and unhedged estimates.

expected.notes: Renegade thesis core — defense + workflow-critical. Brief should be confident on fit score but rigorous on the bear case (regulatory dependency, single-customer concentration on DoD).
Industrial automation, dual-use thesis. Brief should engage with the capital intensity question.
Horizontal banking SaaS — off Renegade's thesis. Brief should NOT score this high on thesis_fit; the bear case should articulate the thesis misalignment, not just generic risks.
